big_picture: Canada Police warned ahead of ‘Khalistan Zindabad’ rallies outside temples, specifically naming the Triveni Mandir Brampton in the Greater Toronto Area and the Lakshmi Narayan Mandir in Surrey, British Columbia. Congress president Kharge stated the FCRA amendment ahead of Kerala Assembly Elections 2026 is an attack on minorities. The ministry of social justice and empowerment introduced an amendment to the Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Act, 2019 on March 13, 2026, which was passed by Parliament on March 25, 2026. The Monetary Policy Committee is expected to hold rates as war-driven risks cloud inflation and growth outlook, according to a Mint poll of 10 economists and market participants. A case was filed against AIMIM MLC and others for obstructing the GHMC anti-encroachment drive, alleging aggressive behavior and interference. Assam Chief Minister Himanta Biswa Sarma threatened to file a defamation case after Congress claimed his wife has passports from three countries. In West Bengal, each judicial officer decided between 12 to 18 objections an hour during the SIR process.

Additionally, the Confederation of Indian Industry (CII) has sought fiscal support from the Finance Ministry amid the West Asia conflict.

Multiple reports suggest the RBI will adopt a cautious "wait-and-watch" approach, while industry bodies like CII have proposed policy actions to address economic challenges. SBI’s report indicates that the MPC, led by Governor Shaktikanta Das, is unlikely to change rates in the first policy decision following the US-Iran conflict.

Civil Liberties.summary: The Parliament passed the Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Amendment Bill, 2026, which removes the right to self-identified gender recognition for transgender persons. Tamil Nadu Chief Minister M.K. Stalin criticized the FCRA amendment, calling it "draconian" and stating it takes away Constitutional rights. Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge alleged the FCRA amendment targets minorities, particularly the Christian community, and suggested an understanding between the LDF and BJP ahead of the Kerala Assembly Elections.
Civil Liberties.top_article_title: The Transgender Persons Amendment Bill, 2026, and its Discontents

Legal.summary: The Supreme Court quashed an NCLAT order for relying on a non-existent quasi-judicial income tax order, requiring the matter to be reconsidered under settled competition law principles. The Election Commission did not specify a tenure for a police chief appointed by it, raising the question of whether a minimum two-year tenure, as mandated by the Supreme Court in Prakash Singh, applies to such appointments. The Kerala High Court closed a PIL against plastic flex banners in poll campaigns and directed the Election Commission and Chief Electoral Officer to enforce the ban on PVC flex and non-recyclable plastic materials under existing government orders and statutory provisions.
Legal.top_article_title: Spillover Effect Dead Ends: SC’s Flipkart Verdict, Quashed AO Orders & Dawn of Income Tax Act 2025

Politics.summary: The Lok Sabha passed the Central Armed Police Forces Bill with a voice vote, prompting Opposition members to protest and walk out. The Lok Sabha cleared a Bill to designate Amaravati as the sole capital of Andhra Pradesh, with the YSR Congress staging a walkout. The Lok Sabha passed a bill declaring Amaravati as the capital of Andhra Pradesh, with support from the Telugu Desam Party, Bharatiya Janata Party, and Congress, while the YSR Congress Party opposed it.
Politics.top_article_title: Lok Sabha passes CAPF Bill with voice vote; Opposition members protest, walk out

Governance.summary: The VDA tax framework in India distinguishes between Sections 115BBH and 43(5) for crypto derivatives, impacting tax rates, deductions, and loss treatment. CropLife India praised the 2026 Plastic Waste Management Amendment Rules for exempting the pesticide industry from mandatory recycled plastic use to align with CIB RC standards. The Delhi government is finalizing a slum rehabilitation policy that includes schools, Jan Suvidha Kendras, and sanitation arrangements for women.
Governance.top_article_title: India’s VDA Tax Framework & CA Professional Obligation (Tax-Efficient Crypto Derivatives)

Finance.summary:
The Reserve Bank of India issued a Master Direction – Facility for Exchange of Notes and Coins under Section 35A of the Banking Regulation Act, 1949, to regulate the exchange of currency notes and coins.

The Reserve Bank of India banned non-deliverable forward (NDF) rupee contracts to tighten foreign exchange rules.

The Reserve Bank of India updated banks on the implementation of Section 51A of the UAPA, 1967, regarding the listing of one entry on the UNSC’s 1267/1989 ISIL (Da’esh) & Al-Qaida Sanctions List.
Finance.top_article_title: Master Direction – Facility for Exchange of Notes and Coins

Foreign.summary:
External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar held talks with Iranian and Gulf leaders to address bilateral ties and regional developments amid West Asia tensions.

Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov is scheduled to visit Delhi in mid-May for a BRICS meeting.

The Reserve Bank of India is likely to keep interest rates unchanged while prioritizing rupee stability and bond yields amid global economic uncertainties.
Foreign.top_article_title: Jaishankar Holds Talks with Iran, Gulf Leaders Amid West Asia Tensions

Energy.summary:
Moody’s reduced India’s FY27 GDP growth forecast to 6% due to disruptions from the West Asia conflict, which supplies 55% of India’s crude oil imports and over 90% of its LPG.

The Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas assured sufficient fuel supplies despite war disruptions, urging consumers to avoid panic buying.

NTPC aims to reach 149 GW capacity by 2032, with Diwakar Kaushik highlighting priorities like commissioning Singrauli Stage-III units and improving gas availability for Dadri and Auraiya plants to boost their Plant Load Factor.
Energy.top_article_title: Moody's cuts India's FY27 GDP growth estimates to 6% amid West Asia conflict

Trade.summary:
The Confederation of Indian Industry proposed a 20-point policy action for the Finance Ministry and Reserve Bank of India, including a temporary three-month moratorium and restructuring window for MSMEs, particularly exporters and ancillary units linked to export supply chains.

The Global Trade Research Initiative reported that India is largely shielded from the impact of a 100% ad valorem duty imposed by U.S. President Trump on certain patented pharmaceuticals and associated ingredients through an executive order.

Union Minister Piyush Goyal stated that extending the WTO e-commerce duty moratorium for a longer period would provide businesses with greater certainty and predictability in their operations.
Trade.top_article_title: CII proposes 20 points policy action for Finmin, RBI to face war challenges

Defence.summary:
The British Military Sees Youth Jobs Crisis as an Opportunity highlights that young Britons facing unemployment are increasingly enlisting in the military, with the government leveraging this trend to address both youth job shortages and military recruitment gaps.

Nigeria–US Military Cooperation and the Challenge of Containing Sahel Instability discusses efforts between Nigeria and the US to strengthen military cooperation amid growing instability in the Sahel region.

BSF considers snakes, crocodiles to curb infiltration along Bangladesh border reports that the Border Security Force is exploring the use of snakes and crocodiles as deterrents to prevent infiltration along the Bangladesh border.
Defence.top_article_title: The British Military Sees Youth Jobs Crisis as an Opportunity

Welfare.summary: The Odisha Cabinet approved an 11.25% quota for Socially and Educationally Backward Classes (SEBC) and increased reservations for Scheduled Castes (SC) and Scheduled Tribes (ST) in medical and technical courses across state institutions. The BJP’s welfare schemes remain a key factor in Assam, overshadowing voter discontent over the delayed justice for singer Zubeen Garg. In Karnataka, 26,767 children registered under the sponsorship scheme by the Women and Child Development Department have not received payments for a year.
Welfare.top_article_title: Odisha cabinet approves 11.25% SEBC quota, hikes SC/ST reservation

Education.summary:
Tamil Nadu Chief Minister M K Stalin criticized the Centre’s three-language formula under the National Education Policy, calling it “Hindi imposition,” while Union Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan described it as “linguistic liberation.”

Odisha Chief Minister Naveen Patnaik announced that the state will increase Scheduled Caste and Scheduled Tribe quotas and introduce Other Backward Classes reservation in medical and technical education across all universities, affiliated colleges, Industrial Training Institutes, and polytechnics.

Jammu and Kashmir Assembly passed a Bill to establish private universities, with former Chief Minister Omar Abdullah calling it a “milestone” for youth.
Education.top_article_title: Stalin slams NEP 3-language plan, Pradhan calls it ‘linguistic liberation’

Infrastructure.summary:
The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) now allows currency exchange at airport departure gates for passengers who forget to do so beforehand.

The Centre has granted stage-I forest clearance for Odisha’s Puri airport project.

The National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) aims to reduce its debt below ₹1.5 trillion in FY27 and become debt-free by 2030.
Infrastructure.top_article_title: Forgot to exchange cash? RBI now lets you do it at airport departure gate

Health.summary:
The mother of the RG Kar Medical College rape and murder victim, who joined the BJP, will contest the West Bengal Assembly elections at Panihati.

Patent expiry has enabled Indian pharmaceutical firms to launch low-cost generics in the anti-obesity drug market, with more options expected soon.

A study by the Centre for Science and Environment found that 99% of India’s disaster days in 2025 were climate-related, yet banks continue to overlook debt linked to these disasters.
Health.top_article_title: Bengal elections 2026: At Panihati, mother of RG Kar victim challenges TMC’s Tirthankar Ghosh

Telecom.summary: India's telecom subscriber base reached 1.32 billion in February 2026, with broadband adoption driving a monthly gain of 7.31 million subscribers. TRAI released Telecom Subscription Data for February 2026, reflecting the sector's growth. Nokia’s report projects India’s 5G subscriber base to surpass 1 billion by 2031, noting average monthly mobile data consumption per user rising to over 31 GB in 2025.
Telecom.top_article_title: TRAI releases Telecom Subscription Data as on February 2026

Technology.summary:
The Finance Ministry cleared over ₹1 lakh crore for India Semiconductor Mission 2.0, with Cabinet approval expected by April-end, focusing on R&D, design, and advanced nodes.

India has two sovereign-AI programmes, one official and another unofficial.

The government set a December 31 deadline for apps to comply with SIM-binding requirements to enhance security and user verification.
Technology.top_article_title: Finance Ministry clears over ₹1 lakh cr for India Semiconductor Mission 2.0; Cabinet nod likely soon

Fintech.summary: The IMF highlights that tokenization could enhance financial inclusion and capital access for emerging economies while also posing risks such as regulatory challenges and market volatility.
Fintech.top_article_title: IMF Flags Opportunities and Risks of Tokenization for Emerging Economies

Agriculture.summary:
India's dairy sector leaders in New Delhi emphasized the need for a climate-resilient ecosystem through integrated value chains, innovative financing, and farmer-centric solutions to secure sustainable rural livelihoods.

The Uttar Pradesh government will begin MSP procurement of gram, lentil, mustard, and tur from April 2 to June 30 at over 190 centres across the state.

Mango supplies in Bengaluru have not reached full capacity due to export uncertainty from the West Asia crisis, with more varieties expected from Andhra Pradesh, Tamil Nadu, and Karnataka in the next 10 days.
Agriculture.top_article_title: Building a climate-resilient and future-ready dairy sector need of the hour

Environment.summary:
Fifty-three captive-bred gharials were released into Kuno National Park’s river in Madhya Pradesh as part of ongoing species conservation efforts.

Governments failed to agree on a timeline for the Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change’s AR7 report after a “frustrating and disappointing” meeting.

Disagreements over the next IPCC assessment cycle have highlighted tensions between nations regarding the scope and timing of future climate reports.

Data Privacy.summary: The USTR warned that rules under India’s Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Act may affect the operations of US credit bureaus in India.
Data Privacy.top_article_title: DPDP Act rules may impact US credit bureaus' India operations: USTR

Labour.summary: Gig worker shortages persist across quick commerce, ecommerce and home service platforms. Operational days in Surat’s textile hub have been reduced to five days a week due to rising input costs amid the West Asia conflict. The All India Bank Employees' Association has requested Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman to order a CBI probe into Atanu Chakraborty’s resignation from HDFC Bank.
Labour.top_article_title: Gig worker shortage continues; IT weathers war jitters

Urban.summary: Residents in Poonamallee, Tamil Nadu, highlight ongoing gaps in civic amenities like sewer systems, roads, and water supply despite infrastructure projects, while DMK MLA Krishnaswamy claims most poll promises have been fulfilled. New real estate tax rules for FY27 include changes to what remains and what becomes more accessible for property owners. A sudden 15-minute rain in Pune caused severe waterlogging and traffic disruptions, with experts attributing the issue to poor drainage and unusual weather patterns.
Urban.top_article_title: T.N. Assembly election 2026: Despite a slew of infrastructure projects, Poonamallee constituency residents point to gaps in civic amenities

DeepTech.summary:
Happiest Minds Technologies is preparing to launch an agentic service delivery platform as its first Agentic AI product, developed over 18 months and positioned as its key differentiator.

India’s IndiaAI Mission has funded 12 AI projects to build sovereign models, with BharatGen receiving over ₹1,000 crore—four times the next highest allocation—and Sarvam AI and BharatGen launching models on AIKosh at Impact Summit 2026.

Anthropic’s AI coding tool Claude Code accidentally exposed its full source code due to a packaging error, though no user data or core AI systems were compromised.
DeepTech.top_article_title: Happiest Minds may launch its first Agentic AI for engineering soon

articles.title: Spillover Effect Dead Ends: SC’s Flipkart Verdict, Quashed AO Orders & Dawn of Income Tax Act 2025
articles.summary: The Supreme Court set aside an NCLAT order that relied on a non-existent income tax quasi-judicial order in a case involving Flipkart. The matter must now be reconsidered from scratch based on established competition law principles. This ruling has implications for tax and regulatory enforcement in India. The decision underscores the need for adherence to legal procedures in tax-related disputes.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court set aside the NCLAT order for relying on a non-existent quasi-judicial income tax order. The key takeaway is that the matter must be reconsidered afresh following settled competition law principles. The post Spillover Effect Dead Ends: SC’s Flipkart Verdict, Quashed AO Orders   Dawn of Income Tax Act 2025 appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: A debate on the tenure of Election Commission-appointed police chief
articles.summary: The Election Commission appointed Mr. Rathore as police chief but did not specify his tenure. The issue arises because the Supreme Court's Prakash Singh case mandates a minimum two-year tenure for police chiefs appointed through the UPSC-led process, raising questions about whether this applies to EC-appointed officials. The debate centers on legal interpretation and potential policy implications for police governance in India.
articles.raw_summary: However, the EC order did not specify a tenure for him. The question is whether Mr. Rathore would be entitled to a minimum two-year tenure, as mandated by the Supreme Court in the landmark Prakash Singh case. However, this ruling applies only to appointments made through the UPSC-led process

Govindan questioned how the UDF would manage state administration if elected in 2026, highlighting concerns over governance and accountability. The remarks reflect political opposition to the UDF's performance and potential demands for policy reforms in disaster management.

articles.title: RBI may keep rates unchanged, focus on rupee stability and bond yields
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India is widely expected to maintain interest rates and its policy stance unchanged due to global economic uncertainties, including the US-Israel conflict impacting energy prices and fiscal deficit concerns. Policymakers will closely monitor the rupee's performance and potential capital outflows, with the RBI possibly considering measures to support the currency without an aggressive immediate response.
articles.raw_summary: The Reserve Bank of India is widely expected to maintain interest rates and its policy stance unchanged due to global economic uncertainties, including the US-Israel conflict impacting energy prices and fiscal deficit concerns. Policymakers will closely monitor the rupee's performance and potential capital outflows, with the RBI possibly considering measures to support the currency without an aggressive immediate response.

articles.title: Kerala likely to see 20% drop in remittances as West Asia war drags on
articles.summary: Kerala is expected to see a 20% drop in remittances this year due to the ongoing war in West Asia. The state heavily relies on these funds, which could ripple through its economy. While currency depreciation may cushion short-term effects, prolonged decline could strain Kerala's financial stability.
articles.raw_summary: Although the depreciation of the currency against the dollar is expected to cushion short-term impact, Kerala is expected to see at least a 20% decline in remittances this year, especially if the war continues.The ramifications of this crisis could ripple through Kerala’s economy, which heavily relies on these funds.

articles.title: Agricultural Income: A Comparative Analysis of Income-tax Act, 1961 vs 2025
articles.summary: The article compares the existing Income-tax Act, 1961 with proposed changes expected by 2025, focusing on agricultural income. The new law aims to tighten conditions for tax exemptions on agricultural income to reduce misuse. It emphasizes stricter documentation and exclusions, which could impact farmers and tax authorities. The analysis is published by TaxGuru.
articles.raw_summary: The new law narrows the scope of agricultural income by introducing strict conditions and exclusions. It emphasizes documentation and limits misuse of tax exemptions. The post Agricultural Income: A Comparative Analysis of Income-tax Act, 1961 vs 2025 appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Section 144C Income Tax Act Amendment: Retrospective Clarification or Legislative Overreach?
articles.summary: The article examines the controversy surrounding a retrospective amendment to Section 144C of India's Income Tax Act. It questions whether the amendment clarifies existing legal ambiguities or overreaches by expanding tax authorities' powers retroactively. The discussion highlights concerns about fairness and legal certainty in tax law, with implications for taxpayers and judicial precedents.
articles.raw_summary: Retrospective amendments in tax law raises concerns about fairness and legal certainty. This blog evaluates the controversy surrounding Section 144C by raising a broader question: does this amendment simply aims to resolve conflicting judicial interpretations, or does it under a guise retrospectively expand the powers of tax authorities in a manner inconsistent with established principles of certainty in taxation? articles.summary: Israel's parliament, the Knesset, passed amendments to a law that mandates the death penalty for Palestinians convicted of terrorism. The changes eliminate the need for proper appeals, pardons, or judicial discretion. The amendments apply specifically to Palestinians with terror convictions and remove safeguards typically present in legal proceedings.

articles.title: ‘Bad in law’: National Green Tribunal stays Assam order to deploy forest officers for poll duty
articles.summary: The National Green Tribunal (NGT) has stayed an Assam government order that directed forest officers to be deployed for election duty. The petitioner argued that the order violated the 2002 Biological Diversity Act and a 2024 Supreme Court ruling prohibiting the use of forest staff for election purposes. The NGT's decision highlights conflicts between electoral processes and environmental governance, raising questions about the legal boundaries of government orders.
articles.raw_summary: The petitioner argued that the order violated the 2002 Biological Diversity Act and a 2024 Supreme Court order against deploying forest staffers for elections.

articles.title: Govt eases fund release rules for centrally sponsored schemes
articles.summary: The Indian government has issued a notification allowing ministries to issue consolidated 'mother sanctions' for multiple centrally sponsored schemes and fund amounts at once, instead of separate approvals. This change aims to streamline fund disbursement and improve administrative flexibility for centrally sponsored schemes. The decision is part of the central government's governance and financial management policies.

articles.title: FEMA SCN Set Aside – SC Holds Adjudication Cannot Override Competent Authority Findings Pending Appeal
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that adjudication proceedings under FEMA cannot override findings by the Competent Authority if no basis for seizure exists. The Court held that such proceedings lack jurisdictional facts and must be set aside, especially when an appeal is pending. This decision impacts how enforcement actions under FEMA are conducted, ensuring they align with the Competent Authority's findings. The ruling underscores the importance of jurisdictional integrity in regulatory enforcement.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court held that where the Competent Authority finds no basis for seizure, the foundation of the SCN collapses. It ruled that proceedings cannot continue without jurisdictional facts. The post FEMA SCN Set Aside   SC Holds Adjudication Cannot Override Competent Authority Findings Pending Appeal appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.summary: The Supreme Court has ordered an inquiry into the widespread illegal conversion of residential areas into commercial zones in state and union territory capitals. articles.title: Section 80P Deduction Allowed on Bank Interest – ITAT Follows Tumkur Merchants, Distinguishes Totagars
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that interest earned on bank deposits made from surplus business funds is considered business income, not income from other sources. This allows businesses to claim tax deductions under Section 80P. The ruling follows a previous decision in Tumkur Merchants' case and distinguishes it from the Totagars case. The decision impacts how businesses classify and tax their interest income.
articles.raw_summary: The ruling holds that deposits made from surplus business funds are part of operational activity. Interest earned on such deposits is business income and not income from other sources. Therefore, Section 80P benefit was granted. The post Section 80P Deduction Allowed on Bank Interest   ITAT Follows Tumkur Merchants, Distinguishes Totagars appeared first on TaxGuru .

The policy aims to standardize taxation for EVs while generating revenue for the government.

articles.title: No 143(2) Notice, No Reassessment: ITAT Quashes Proceedings Despite Delayed ITR
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) quashed income tax reassessment proceedings against a taxpayer because the tax department failed to issue a mandatory notice under Section 143(2) of the Income Tax Act. The ruling highlights that the absence of such a notice renders the entire assessment process invalid, setting a precedent for future tax disputes. The case involved a delayed income tax return (ITR) filing, but the tribunal prioritized procedural compliance over the delay.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held the assessment invalid as no mandatory notice under Section 143(2) was issued. The key takeaway is that absence of such notice renders the entire assessment void. articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal ruled that mismatches in tax filings under GST Section 74 do not automatically imply fraudulent intent. The decision underscores the need for intent to evade tax, not just procedural errors, in tax disputes.

articles.summary: The rate of interest on Government of India Floating Rate Bond 2028 (FRB 2028) applicable for the half year April 04, 2026 to October 03, 2026 shall be 6.17 per cent per annum. It may be recalled that FRB 2028 carries a coupon, which has a base rate equivalent to the average of the Weighted Average Yield (WAY) of last three auctions (from the rate fixing day i.e., April 04, 2026) of 182 Day T-Bills, plus a fixed spread (0.64 per cent). articles.title: GST Penalty Order Quashed for Passing Before Reply Deadline Expired: P&H HC
articles.summary: The Punjab & Haryana High Court set aside a GST penalty order issued by authorities before the taxpayer's reply deadline had expired. The court ruled that such an order violates natural justice principles, even if the penalty was paid. Authorities must now consider objections and pass a reasoned order. The decision underscores the importance of procedural fairness in tax proceedings.
articles.raw_summary: The Court held that issuing an order before the reply deadline violates natural justice. Even if penalty is paid, authorities must consider objections and pass a reasoned order. articles.title: Legal Research, Understanding Impugned Order No Ground To Condone Delay: Delhi High Court
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court has held that conducting legal research or understanding the impugned order cannot be a ground for condoning the delay. Justice Swarana Kanta Sharma said that if such a ground were to be accepted as a sufficient explanation, it would render the law of limitation and the principles of delay and laches largely otiose. articles.title: Ex-Parte GST Order on Wrong ITC Availment Set Aside Due to Lack of Opportunity to Respond
articles.summary: The court overturned GST orders issued against a taxpayer for wrongly availing Input Tax Credit (ITC), ruling that the proceedings were ex-parte and violated the taxpayer's right to respond to a show cause notice before adjudication. The decision emphasizes the importance of due process in tax administration. The original and appellate authorities had not provided the taxpayer an opportunity to reply, leading to the orders being set aside.
articles.raw_summary: The court set aside GST orders after finding both original and appellate proceedings were effectively ex-parte. It held that the taxpayer must be given an opportunity to reply to the show cause notice before adjudication. articles.title: Share Gains Treated as Capital Gains Due to Consistent Treatment
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that share gains should be treated as capital gains, not business income, based on the investor’s consistent tax treatment and factual findings. The Revenue Department (tax authorities) had appealed the ITAT’s decision, arguing that the transactions constituted business income. The Court upheld the investor’s status, dismissing the Revenue’s appeal and reinforcing the principle of factual consistency in tax classification.
articles.raw_summary: The issue was whether share transactions constituted business income or capital gains. The Court upheld investor status based on consistent treatment and factual findings, dismissing the Revenue’s appeal. articles.title: Clubbing of Income under Income Tax Act, 1961 (Sections 60 to 64)
articles.summary: The article explains provisions under India's Income Tax Act, 1961 (Sections 60 to 64) that prevent tax avoidance by ensuring income transferred indirectly without transferring the underlying asset remains taxable in the transferor's hands. These rules aim to close loopholes where individuals might try to reduce their tax liability by shifting income to others. The provisions are part of India's tax policy framework to maintain fairness in income taxation.
articles.raw_summary: Income transferred without transferring the underlying asset remains taxable in the hands of the transferor. The provisions ensure that tax liability cannot be avoided through indirect income transfers. The post Clubbing of Income under Income Tax Act, 1961 (Sections 60 to 64) appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: NTPC eyes 149 GW by 2032, expands renewable and nuclear footprint in UP: Diwakar Kaushik

The company aims to expand its total capacity to 149 GW by 2032, focusing on renewable and nuclear energy. These efforts support India's broader energy transition and infrastructure development goals.

These include the timely commissioning of the Singrauli Stage-III units, improving gas availability for the Dadri and Auraiya gas-based plants to enhance their Plant Load Factor (PLF), and ensuring the on-schedule execution of solar projects across Uttar Pradesh.

The court’s intervention underscores the urgency and legal authority behind the revival efforts.
articles.raw_summary: A Supreme Court-appointed panel has begun steps to revive Noida’s stalled Supernova project, appointing EY as forensic auditor and starting homebuyer verification to fast-track resolution.

articles.title: Draft IT amendments risk over-censorship, over-compliance, say experts
articles.summary: Experts warn that India's draft IT Rules amendments could lead to over-censorship and over-compliance. The proposed changes expand state power, make government advisories legally binding, and broaden content takedown requirements. The public consultation for these amendments is open until April 14. The rules risk impacting free speech, surveillance practices, and digital platform operations in India.

The US has set a Tuesday deadline for the strait to reopen, while missile strikes and air raids have further intensified the regional crisis. This situation could impact global oil supplies and India's energy security, given its reliance on crude imports through the strait.
articles.raw_summary: Iran-US-Israel War Live News Updates: The Iran-US-Israel war intensified after Iran shot down US military aircraft and a pilot went missing, while missile strikes, air raids and Strait of Hormuz tensions further escalated the regional conflict.

articles.title: Premium petrol and diesel prices rise amid crude surge: What it means for consumers
articles.summary: Premium petrol and diesel prices in India have risen from April 1, with XP100 petrol and premium diesel becoming costlier. Aviation Turbine Fuel (ATF) rates were revised after a sharp spike, and commercial LPG prices have increased by over ₹300 since March. The surge in crude oil prices is the primary driver of these changes, which will impact consumer costs and potentially trigger government responses like subsidies or price controls.
articles.raw_summary: Premium fuel prices have increased from April 1, with XP100 petrol and premium diesel becoming costlier. ATF rates were revised after a sharp spike, while commercial LPG prices have risen by over ₹300 since March.

articles.title: Petition filed in SC challenging amended 'transgender persons' law
articles.summary: Transgender rights activists have filed a petition in the Supreme Court challenging the constitutional validity of the Transgender Persons (Protection of Rights) Amendment Act, 2026. The amendments remove self-identified gender as the basis for transgender certificates and introduce a medical board, departing from the NALSA judgment's recognition of personal dignity and choice. The petition argues the changes violate fundamental rights. The case highlights ongoing debates over transgender rights

The amendments remove self-perceived identity as the basis for TG certificates and introduce a medical board, moving away from the NALSA judgment's affirmation of personal dignity and choice.

articles.title: Lawyers Have Some Dignity And They Cannot Be Treated Like A Servant: Rajasthan HC
articles.summary: The Rajasthan High Court ruled that the termination of assistant advocates without following proper procedure was arbitrary and unlawful. The court reinstated the advocates and directed the formulation of fair guidelines for their disengagement. The ruling emphasized that lawyers cannot be treated like servants and must be accorded dignity in their employment terms. The decision underscores the judiciary's role in protecting legal professionals' rights and ensuring due process in termination cas
articles.raw_summary: The Court examined whether termination of Assistant Advocates was carried out lawfully. It held that disengagement without following proper procedure and terms is arbitrary. The ruling reinstated the advocates and mandated fair guidelines. The post Lawyers Have Some Dignity And They Cannot Be Treated Like A Servant: Rajasthan HC appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Bahrain postpones vote on UN resolution aimed at ending Iran's stranglehold on Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: Bahrain has postponed a UN Security Council vote on a resolution aimed at reopening the Strait of Hormuz, a key oil shipping route. The vote was delayed due to opposition from Russia and China, with a new date expected next week. The resolution seeks to address Iran's influence over the strait, which is vital for global oil trade. The delay highlights ongoing geopolitical tensions affecting international maritime security.
articles.raw_summary: Bahrain delays UN Security Council vote on a watered down resolution to reopen the Strait of Hormuz after opposition from Russia and China, vote expected next week.

articles.title: Addition Under Section 40A(2)(a) Deleted Due to Incorrect Comparison of Services
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that the Assessing Officer incorrectly compared dissimilar email marketing services to determine excess payments under Section 40A(2)(a) of the Income Tax Act. The tribunal clarified that only comparable services can be used for arm’s length evaluations, leading to the deletion of the tax addition. This ruling impacts how tax authorities assess cross-border transactions and transfer pricing for services.
articles.raw_summary: The ITAT held that the Assessing Officer wrongly compared dissimilar email marketing services to determine excess payment. The ruling clarifies that only comparable services can be used for arm’s length evaluation, leading to deletion of the addition. The post Addition Under Section 40A(2)(a) Deleted Due to Incorrect Comparison of Services appeared first on TaxGuru .

It also mentions that raising fuel prices could help manage the fiscal deficit. The analysis emphasizes the need for a neutral approach to monetary and fiscal policies.

Raising fuel prices can help manage the fiscal deficit.

The Left parties are rallying behind him, emphasizing there is no alternative to his leadership. This election could shape the state's governance and policy priorities in the coming years.

articles.title: Left leans on towering Vijayan in uphill battle to keep Kerala

The article highlights the political dynamics in Kerala, where Vijayan's leadership is central to the LDF's efforts to retain power. The outcome of this election could shape the state's policy priorities, particularly around governance and development initiatives.
articles.raw_summary: At 81, Vijayan is chasing history; and his message is “vikasanam” (development).

articles.title: A Post-American Persian Gulf?
articles.summary: The article examines how the ongoing Iran war could accelerate economic and geopolitical changes in the Persian Gulf. The conflict may reshape regional trade, energy flows, and security dynamics, which could indirectly impact India's energy imports, maritime interests, and diplomatic strategies. The analysis suggests that these shifts may require India to reassess its policies in the Middle East.
articles.raw_summary: The Iran war will accelerate the region’s economic transformation.

articles.title: US targets Chinese chipmaking with proposed export restrictions on ASML and others
articles.summary: The US proposed the draft MATCH Act to restrict exports of advanced chipmaking tools to China, aiming to maintain its lead in artificial intelligence. The restrictions would apply to companies like ASML and extend to US-allied nations. The move seeks to prevent China from accessing technologies it cannot produce domestically. This could impact global semiconductor supply chains and India's technology and trade policies.
articles.raw_summary: The draft MATCH Act announced late on Thursday is aimed at protecting the US ​lead in artificial intelligence by preventing Chinese companies from obtaining chip manufacturing tools they ​cannot make themselves and ensuring that companies based in US-allied nations face the same restrictions as their US rivals.

articles.title: Key petrochemical products get 3 month import duty relief
articles.summary: India has waived customs duty on key petrochemical imports until June 30. This move provides relief to sectors like pharmaceuticals, chemicals, and textiles. The government aims to ensure stable domestic supplies and control prices. This exemption is a temporary measure to support industries and consumers.

articles.title: US tightens chip export controls, curb China's tech rise
articles.summary: The US has tightened export controls on semiconductor manufacturing equipment to curb China's technological advancement. A committee stated that semiconductors are central to global tech competition and introduced the MATCH Act to strengthen these controls and coordinate a global approach. The move aims to restrict China's access to advanced chipmaking tools, which could impact global supply chains and India's tech industry.
articles.raw_summary: The committee further stated that semiconductor manufacturing lies at the core of the technological competition with China, adding that the MATCH Act is designed to strengthen export controls and create a more coordinated global approach, as highlighted in its release.

articles.title: In 5-point proposal to end West Asia war, Pakistan & China urge ‘cessation of hostilities, peace talks’
articles.summary: Pakistan and China have proposed a five-point plan to end the ongoing war in West Asia, urging a cessation of hostilities and peace talks. Islamabad has been acting as a mediator between the US and Iran for over a week to push for regional stability. Iran has not yet accepted the proposed framework for discussions. The proposal highlights diplomatic efforts to address the conflict, which could impact India's energy imports and regional security.
articles.raw_summary: For over a week, Islamabad has been conveying messages between US   Iran and making coordinated push for regional stability. Iran has said it hasn’t accepted framework for discussions.

articles.title: Geopolitics overtakes economics as key driver of global trade flows: Experts
articles.summary: Experts warn that geopolitical tensions in West Asia—particularly around the Strait of Hormuz and Red Sea—are disrupting global trade. Instability in these maritime corridors risks supply chain breakdowns, higher freight costs, and delayed shipments, which could impact India’s energy security and trade flows. The article underscores how regional conflicts may force India to reassess its trade and energy policies.
articles.raw_summary: Instability in West Asia, especially near vital maritime corridors like the Strait of Hormuz and the Red Sea, has heightened worries about supply chain disruptions, rising freight costs, and delayed shipments

articles.title: Iran war: Natural gas pool set to release 7 mmscmd to priority sectors amid supply crunch
articles.summary: Iran is set to release 7 million metric standard cubic meters per day (mmscmd) of natural gas from a pooled supply to priority sectors like city gas distributors and fertilizer manufacturers. The gas will be priced at around $11.60 per mmbtu. This move comes amid a supply shortage and could influence India's energy imports and policy considerations.
articles.raw_summary: City gas distributors, fertiliser makers, and other users will receive these supplies. The pooled gas will be priced at around $11.60 per mmbtu.

articles.title: French-owned ship passes through Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: A ship owned by a major French company has passed through the Strait of Hormuz, a critical oil transit route, for the first time since the conflict in the region began. The Strait of Hormuz is a vital chokepoint for global oil shipments, and its stability is crucial for India's energy imports. The event highlights ongoing geopolitical tensions and their potential impact on maritime security and oil supply chains.
articles.raw_summary: It appears to be the first ship owned by a major European firm to go through the strait since the conflict began.

articles.title: $15 billion investment: Google to kick off data centre project on April 28
articles.summary: Google is set to launch a $15 billion data centre project in Visakhapatnam on April 28. The project, expected to be Asia's largest, will expand Google's global data centre presence to 12 countries. The initiative highlights India's growing role in the global digital infrastructure landscape and could influence policies related to data storage, digital economy, and regional development.
articles.raw_summary: So far, Google and its subsidiaries have data centres in 11 countries - the United States, Taiwan, Japan, Singapore, Ireland, the Netherlands, Denmark, Finland, Germany, Belgium, and Chile, spread across 29 geographic locations. The data centre cluster in Visakhapatnam is expected to be Asia's largest.

The Court emphasized the importance of seizing rare opportunities. The ruling involves a candidate and the public employment selection process, with implications for fairness and merit-based hiring in government jobs.
articles.raw_summary: 'When chances are rare, one needs to grab them with both hands,' the Court said.

The situation may have implications for India's trade and energy security, given its reliance on Middle Eastern routes for oil and exports.

articles.title: Coal production from captive, commercial mines up 10.2% in FY 2025-26
articles.summary: Coal production from captive and commercial mines in India increased by 10.2% in FY 2025-26. This rise is partly driven by coal being sought as an alternative to natural gas, whose supplies are strained due to escalating tensions in West Asia. The Hindu Economy reports the production growth, highlighting its significance for India's energy security and policy considerations.
articles.raw_summary: Coal is being increasingly sought as an alternative for natural gas, supplies and demand of which find themselves stretched amidst the escalating tensions in West Asia.

This effort is part of a long-term conservation plan aimed at rewilding rhinos in the grasslands of the reserve.

articles.title: NeSL enabling real-time access to digital debt & claims data in insolvency cases
articles.summary: NeSL (National E-Governance Services Limited) is now providing instant access to digital default records for insolvency cases in India, enabling authorities to quickly verify debt and claims. This follows amendments to the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code that designate NeSL as the central gateway for all insolvency proceedings. NeSL is also developing electronic insurance surety bonds to support MSMEs and aims to build digital infrastructure for high-trust transactions.

NeSL aims to build digital infrastructure for high-trust transactions.

articles.title: Trump vows strikes on Iran’s power plants, bridges on Tuesday if Strait of Hormuz stays shut
articles.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump has threatened to launch strikes on Iranian power plants and bridges if Iran continues to block the Strait of Hormuz, a vital oil shipping route. The warning escalates tensions between the U.S. and Iran, raising concerns about potential disruptions to global oil supplies. The Strait of Hormuz is a key chokepoint for oil exports, including those to India, making this development significant for energy security.

articles.summary: The Arunachal Pradesh government distributed six yaks—four females and two males—to local farmers in the Shi-Yomi district as part of a program to revive yak-based income for tribal communities. The initiative aims to boost livelihoods in Mechuka by reintroducing yaks, which are culturally and economically significant to the region. The program is being implemented in collaboration with local authorities to support tribal farmers.
articles.raw_summary: As part of the programme, six yak—including four females and two males—were distributed to local farmers to help revive yak-based income in the Shi-Yomi district.

articles.title: US Allies Work On Plan B For Hormuz Strait If Trump Walks Away
articles.summary: More than 40 US allies, including key partners, met to discuss contingency plans for reopening the Strait of Hormuz. The move signals deep concern over the waterway's crisis, triggered by US-Iran tensions under President Trump's policies. The Strait is a critical global oil transit route, and any disruption could impact energy supplies. India, heavily reliant on oil imports, may face indirect consequences from such geopolitical developments.
articles.raw_summary: More than 40 US allies met Thursday to discuss plans to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, in a signal to President Donald Trump of the deep concern across the international community about the crisis in the waterway triggered by his war on Iran.

A mix of global influences and local policies has resulted in a weakened rupee, affecting everyday expenses for citizens, the financial burden of education, and the overall fiscal health of the government.

articles.title: Prices of rice, wheat, atta and pulses fall despite West Asia war
articles.summary: Despite the West Asia war causing potential supply disruptions, the prices of essential food items like rice, wheat, atta, and pulses in India have fallen. Consumer Affairs Secretary Nidhi Khare attributed this stability to timely government interventions, including buffer stock releases and supply-side measures. The article underscores how policy actions prevented sharp price spikes in critical food commodities.
articles.raw_summary: Timely interventions, including buffer stock releases and supply-side measures, prevented any sharp spikes despite external shocks, says consumer affairs secretary Nidhi Khare.

articles.title: Oil prices are still high. Why that could actually help stocks and lower inflation.
articles.summary: Oil prices remain high due to geopolitical tensions linked to the Iran war, which is slowing global demand growth. According to 22V Research, this demand slowdown could help reduce inflation and extend the economic cycle. The article highlights how elevated fuel costs may impact India's economy, though no immediate policy action is discussed.
articles.raw_summary: Elevated oil prices tied to the Iran war are weighing on growth, but 22V Research says the slowdown in demand could help lower inflation and extend the economic cycle.

articles.title: Infrastructure boom drives rail freight to record 1,670 mt in FY26 as steel, cement loading rises
articles.summary: The Indian Railways transported a record 1,670 million tonnes of freight in FY26, driven largely by spikes in fertilizer and steel shipments. Despite the milestone, growth was modest, trailing that of other transport sectors and sparking concern about operational efficiency.

articles.title: Urban households mostly use LPG, while rural households rely on firewood, too
articles.summary: A majority of Indian families now use liquefied petroleum gas for cooking, up from just 20% two decades ago. However, a substantial share of households, particularly in poorer states and in rural areas, are still reliant on firewood.

articles.title: India tightens footwear rules to curb toxic chemicals, aid exports push
articles.summary: According to a BIS notification, reviewed by Mint, the new norms prescribe test methods to measure the presence of dimethylformamide and polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons, respectively. Both substances are commonly used in footwear manufacturing and can pose health risks with prolonged exposure.

articles.title: The British Military Sees Youth Jobs Crisis as an Opportunity
articles.summary: Young people in Britain facing high unemployment are increasingly joining the military. The British government is leveraging this trend to address both its armed forces' recruitment shortfall and youth joblessness. The military sees this as an opportunity to strengthen its ranks while providing employment to desperate job seekers.
articles.raw_summary: Young Britons in desperate search of a job are increasingly signing up to the military, with the government looking to exploit a trend that could help address both its youth unemployment crisis and depleted armed forces.

articles.title: Meloni Visits Gulf to Bolster Energy Ties to War-Hit States
articles.summary: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni made a surprise two-day visit to Saudi Arabia to meet leaders, including Crown Prince Mohammed Bin Salman, aiming to bolster energy ties with war-affected Gulf states. The visit is part of efforts to enhance Italy's domestic energy security amid global supply disruptions. The move could influence India's energy import strategies and foreign policy considerations in the region.

articles.title: Gold rate drops on MCX as dollar rises amid US-Iran war; strong US jobs data dents US Fed rate cut hopes
articles.summary: Gold and silver prices on the Multi Commodity Exchange (MCX) fell by nearly 1% each, with gold dropping ₹1,400 to ₹1,48,298 per 10 grams and silver declining over ₹2,800 to ₹2,29,651 per kg. The decline was driven by a stronger US dollar amid the US-Iran conflict and strong US jobs data, which reduced expectations of a US Federal Reserve interest rate cut.

articles.title: Silver rate today loses 1% as dollar strengthens, Fed rate-cut hopes fade amid Middle East war
articles.summary: Silver rates fell on April 6, driven by a stronger U.S. dollar and rising Treasury yields, despite heightened Middle East tensions. On MCX, silver dropped 1% to ₹2,30,465 per kg, while gold decreased by 0.7% to ₹1,48,633 per 10 grams.

articles.summary: Gold prices fell as rising Middle East tensions increased inflation risks and a surprise drop in US jobless claims reduced expectations of US interest rate cuts. The escalation in the war and US labor market data are key drivers of this trend, which could impact global and Indian financial markets, including the Reserve Bank of India's policy decisions.

articles.title: STT hike on futures and options takes effect from today — Here's all you need to know
articles.summary: The Securities Transaction Tax (STT) on equity futures and options has been increased by SEBI, effective immediately. The STT on equity options premium rose from 0.1% to 0.15%, on equity futures from 0.02% to 0.05%, and on equity options exercise from 0.125% to 0.15%. This change aims to adjust tax rates on derivative trading, impacting investors and market participants.
articles.raw_summary: STT on equity options premium has been raised to 0.15% from 0.1% earlier. The STT on equity futures has been raised to 0.05% from 0.02% earlier, while the STT on equity options exercise has been raised to 0.15% from 0.125% earlier.

articles.title: Energy shocks come and go, India’s strength runs deeper
articles.summary: The key takeaway is not that India is insulated from global shocks, but that it is better equipped to manage them, with stress concentrated rather than systemic, buffers meaningful but finite, and policy flexibility available but requiring judicious use, writes Bhavdeep Bhatt, CEO, Northern Arc Investment Managers.

articles.summary: The U.S. Federal Communications Commission (FCC) in 2021 added Huawei, ZTE, Hytera, Hikvision, and Dahua to its 'Covered List' of companies deemed a national security risk. This move could influence global supply chains and regulatory approaches to foreign technology, including in India's telecom and surveillance sectors.

articles.summary: Vedanta Limited reported record FY26 production across aluminium, alumina and zinc, with alumina output surging 48% and mined metal at Zinc India hitting an all-time high. Power sales and copper output also posted strong growth. However, oil and gas production declined 16% year-on-year, weighing on overall performance.

articles.title: World food price rise set to continue if Iran war lasts, FAO says
articles.summary: The UN's Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) reported a 2.4% rise in its global food price index in March, marking a 1% increase from last year. The index remains nearly 20% below its 2022 peak following the Ukraine war. Analysts warn that prolonged conflict in Iran could further drive up food prices. The situation may prompt India to reassess its agricultural policies or trade strategies to mitigate food inflation.
articles.raw_summary: The FAO Food Price Index, which measures changes in a basket of globally traded food commodities, rose by 2.4% from its revised February level. It is 1% above its value a year ago, although nearly 20% below its March 2022 peak, reached after the start of the war in Ukraine.

articles.title: PM Modi arrives in Puducherry, commences roadshow
articles.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi arrived here on Friday to a rousing welcome from the BJP workers amid chants of 'Bharat Mata Ki Jai' and 'Jai Shri Ram,' and he commenced his roadshow in the city ahead of April 9 elections to the territorial Assembly.

This unprecedented increase affects Pakistani consumers and businesses. The situation highlights the global energy crisis and its potential ripple effects on neighboring countries like India, raising concerns about inflation and energy security.
articles.raw_summary: Pakistanis faced record fuel price increases Friday, as petrol and diesel prices rose by up to 54% driven by the war in the Middle East that has caused global oil prices to surge.

The situation could have implications for India's energy security and policy responses.

articles.title: Will crude oil prices tank? Someone has bet nearly $1 billion on this via leverage
articles.summary: Investors have placed nearly $1 billion in leveraged bets on a potential decline in crude oil prices. The United States Oil Fund (USO) and United States Brent Oil Fund (BNO) saw record inflows of $700 million and $600 million respectively in March, the highest since the pandemic. These movements reflect market sentiment and could impact India's energy costs and inflation, given the country's heavy reliance on oil imports.
articles.raw_summary: Its not just the bearish bets, even the bullish funds have drawn record money. The United States Oil Fund (USO) attracted flows worth nearly $700 million in March, the most since the pandemic, while the United States Brent Oil Fund (BNO) saw flows worth a record $600 million.

articles.title: Court grants three-day police custody of director Ranjith in sexual harassment case
articles.summary: A court has granted three-day police custody to authorities investigating Malayalam director Ranjith on allegations of sexual harassment. The case involves serious accusations that could lead to legal proceedings. Police custody allows investigators to question the accused during the specified period.
articles.raw_summary: Malayalam director Ranjith faces serious allegations of sexual harassment, leading to his arrest and three-day police custody.

articles.summary: “When there’s no certainty if the drone will fly or not, why take risk of flying it in populated area?” said ex-CM; police later said it belonged to a pvt firm

articles.title: ‘Economic oppression’: Public outcry in Pakistan as petrol hits PKR 459 per litre
articles.summary: Pakistan's fuel prices surged to PKR 459 per litre, sparking public outrage over economic hardship. Citizens argue the unprecedented increase will severely impact purchasing power. The government's decision reflects broader economic challenges in the region, which could have implications for neighboring countries like India.
articles.raw_summary: Citizens expressed frustration, arguing that the "unprecedented and uncalled-for increase" would cripple the purchasing power of the average person.

articles.title: What are JASSM-ER cruise missiles? Long-range weaponry deployed by US for Iran war with 930 km reach
articles.summary: The US has deployed JASSM-ER cruise missiles, which have a range of 930 km and cost $1.5 million each, for potential use in a conflict with Iran. These missiles are designed to strike both fixed and relocatable targets from a significant distance. The deployment highlights US military capabilities in the region, which may have implications for India's defence and foreign policy considerations.
articles.raw_summary: This weapon system, which costs $1.5 million, is designed to destroy both fixed, and relocatable targets from a significant stand-off distance.

articles.title: ETtech Explainer: Agentic harness, the software that makes AI tick
articles.summary: Indian enterprises are increasingly adopting AI agents for complex business workflows, but the crucial 'agentic harness' is emerging as the key differentiator. This software framework enables AI models to act autonomously and safely, managing memory, tools, and human oversight, becoming the most contested layer in the AI value chain.
